findings.title: /api/jobs/daily-briefing executes full LLM + Firestore-write workload anonymously in prod
findings.evidence: Anon POST returned 200 {"ok":true,"posted":3,"scrapedTotal":150,"durationMs":87088} on 2026-06-06 from the public internet.
findings.money_impact: Each run = ~$1-3 of Gemini + translation. Looped = monthly bill annihilation. Plus Firestore writes to daily_briefings and dedup poisoning of legitimate news.
findings.root_cause: Source has correct CRON_SECRET gate, but live prod is running without it triggering. Either CRON_SECRET not bound to env (consistent with F1-05) or deploy artifact mismatch.
findings.fix_recommendation: Bind CRON_SECRET via Secret Manager. Redeploy. Add audit-deployments.sh probe asserting 401 on this path.

findings.title: /api/jobs/grow-persona-pool creates AI personas anonymously in prod
/api/jobs/ai-community-agent posts AI-persona content anonymously in prod
findings.evidence: Anon POST returned 200 with actions {staffRoomChats:["Mohammed Farooq","Anjali Patil"], groupPost:"Joseph Vargese", likes:"Gurpreet Kaur"} on 2026-06-06.
findings.fix_recommendation: Same as F1-01. Plus consider rate limit at edge — current code path makes multiple Gemini calls per request.

findings.title: Twilio ACCOUNT_SID + AUTH_TOKEN stored as plaintext Cloud Run env vars (not Secret Manager)
findings.evidence: gcloud run services describe sahayakai-hotfix-resilience returns {name:TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN, value:<32-hex>} not valueFrom.secretKeyRef. Contrasts with GOOGLE_GENAI_API_KEY, FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_KEY, SAHAYAKAI_AGENTS_AUDIENCE, SAHAYAKAI_REQUEST_SIGNING_KEY which are all correctly Secret-Manager-bound.
findings.fix_recommendation: Move to Secret Manager. Rotate Twilio auth token immediately (assume compromised since it has been visible to any principal with run.services.get for the deployment lifetime).

findings.title: CRON_SECRET not configured in prod environment
findings.evidence: Anon POST returns 500 {"error":"CRON_SECRET not configured"} — explicit unconfigured branch fires. So legitimate Cloud Scheduler invocations cannot authenticate either; reconciliation is silently broken.
findings.fix_recommendation: Generate 32-byte random secret; bind via Secret Manager + Cloud Scheduler Authorization header.

findings.title: syncUserAction trusts client-supplied email/displayName/photoURL without ID-token cross-check
findings.evidence: Source: dbAdapter.updateUser(callerUid, { uid, email: user.email || '', displayName: user.displayName || '', photoURL: user.photoURL || '' }). uid is verified against session — but email/displayName/photoURL are written verbatim from the client.
findings.attack: Caller authed as attacker@x.com calls syncUserAction({uid: SELF, email: 'ceo@school.com', ...}). users/{SELF}.email now displays as ceo@school.com in teacher directory, community profiles, support tickets, and any feature that reads users.email. Token claim remains attacker@x.com so auth still works for the attacker.
findings.fix_recommendation: Middleware already decodes the ID token — extend it to inject x-user-email (from decoded.email). Server-side in syncUserAction, ignore the client-supplied email and use header. Same treatment for displayName/photoURL (or fetch via Admin SDK auth.getUser(uid)).

findings.title: /api/jobs/ai-reactive-reply auth is fail-open when AI_INTERNAL_SECRET env var unset
findings.evidence: Code: `if (secret) { check }` — if AI_INTERNAL_SECRET is unset, the entire auth check is skipped. Live: anon POST passed auth, only rejected by payload validation (400 'invalid path').
findings.fix_recommendation: Change to: if (!secret || provided !== secret) return 403. Grep the rest of the repo for similar `if (secret)` and `if (cronSecret)` fail-open anti-patterns.

findings.title: /api/jobs/edu-news reachable anonymously
findings.evidence: Anon POST returned 500 with a downstream fetch failure — auth was not the rejection layer. The handler also has no explicit CRON_SECRET gate.
findings.fix_recommendation: Add CRON_SECRET gate matching sibling jobs. Treat as P0 once F1-01/02/03 are fixed because the same exploit class applies.

findings.title: Prod Cloud Run service has roles/run.invoker bound to allUsers
findings.evidence: gcloud run services get-iam-policy sahayakai-hotfix-resilience returns members: [allUsers]. By design for public Next.js, but it disables the platform-level OIDC check the cron-jobs documentation relies on ("Cloud Run validates automatically").
findings.fix_recommendation: Either split cron endpoints onto a second Cloud Run service with IAM-only invoker (cleanest), or require every /api/jobs/* handler to parse-and-verify the Cloud Scheduler OIDC token against the expected SA email.

findings.title: Sidecar audience drift — cannot read SAHAYAKAI_AGENTS_AUDIENCE secret with available IAM
findings.evidence: Sidecar IAM correctly limited to 640589855975-compute@developer (only the compute SA can invoke). Audience secret correctly bound to prod env via valueFrom.secretKeyRef. Literal secret value unverifiable under impersonated firebase-adminsdk SA (PERMISSION_DENIED on secretmanager.versions.access).
findings.code_ref: src/lib/sidecar/*-client.ts (10 client files all use AUDIENCE_ENV = SAHAYAKAI_AGENTS_AUDIENCE)
findings.fix_recommendation: Add CI smoke test: mint OIDC with audience env value, hit sidecar, assert 200. Would catch any future audience drift like the Q4B regression.

verified_correct: Middleware unconditionally strips x-user-id + x-user-plan from inbound headers BEFORE any branch — src/middleware.ts:99-101
Live confirmed: spoofed x-user-id header → 401 Unauthorized on /api/usage
Firebase ID token verified against Google certs with audience+issuer checks (5h cert cache) — src/middleware.ts:62-86
Server-action POST gate enforces auth on page mutations (closes the 'expired session → 500 leak' from 2026-06-01 audit) — src/middleware.ts:202-245
requireAuth() logs WARN before throwing UnauthorizedError when header missing (defense in depth + observability) — src/lib/auth-helpers.ts:31-46
App Check verification on /api/ai/* when APP_CHECK_REQUIRED=true (currently not enabled in prod) — src/middleware.ts:161-176
syncUserAction uid cross-check: returns Forbidden when supplied uid ≠ session uid — src/app/actions/auth.ts:30-43
getUserProfileAction rejects reading another user's profile — src/app/actions/auth.ts:80-87
Razorpay webhook signature verification with throw → 401 (no Razorpay retry storm) — src/app/api/webhooks/razorpay/route.ts:18-32
/api/jobs/billing-reconciliation correctly rejects bad-bearer with 401 (gate functional even though CRON_SECRET unset means it's also blocking real cron)
/api/auth/profile-check correctly de-conflated 'doc exists' vs 'onboarding complete' (2026-04-30 fix in place)
Sidecar IAM is tight: only compute SA can invoke — anon callers cannot impersonate the sidecar even with a stolen audience
AI flows trust only x-user-id from middleware-injected header, not from request body — verified across assistant, lesson-plan/stream, virtual-field-trip, voice-to-text, content/*
